org: London Borough of Barking and Dagenham

candidates.stmt:
I'm honoured to be a councillor candidate for Barking Abbey ward from the Conservative Party. Having lived in Barking for sixteen years, I understand the unique challenges our streets face every day. We deserve better. My goal is simple: a stronger, safer, and cleaner neighbourhood where every resident feels heard. On 7th May 2026, you have a choice. Vote for a candidate who will fight for a council tax freeze, tackle anti-social behaviour, lower your parking charges and always put your voice first.
Your Borough. Your vote. Your Voice.

Alibon deserves better.

I've lived in Dagenham since 2004, and I'm standing to be your councillor because residents are being let down.

Higher council tax. Dirtier streets. Rising parking fees. No accountability.

I'm not a politician - I'm a safety‑critical professional who wants to bring responsibility and real representation back to Alibon ward.

It's time for a councillor who listens and acts.

It's time for change in Alibon.

We need:

* Safer, Clean Streets and Parks.
* A freeze in Council Tax
* Reversal of the 51% increase in the cost of Residents Parking Permits.
* Only Essential CPZs (Controlled Parking Zones)

I am a proud resident of Barking & Dagenham since 2013, living in Riverside with my family. With children in local schools, I understand the needs of our community.
As a Chartered Accountant, I have supported residents with free tax and financial advice. I will use my experience to improve council spending, freeze council tax, reduce crime and introduce Controlled Parking Zones only where needed.
I am standing as your councillor candidate for the Barking Riverside ward to be a strong and dedicated voice for local residents.

I'm proud to stand for Riverside Ward. I've been working at Barking Market for 10 years, and through that I've got to know so many people in our community. I care about helping others, listening to residents, and doing what I can to support everyone. I want to bring people together and help make Riverside an even better place to live and all-inclusive.

I have proudly lived in the United Kingdom since 2016. I work as a carer, supporting vulnerable people in our community every day, which has taught me compassion, patience and the importance of strong local services.

I am standing as a Conservative candidate for Barking Riverside ward councillor because I am passionate about improving our area, keeping our streets safe and clean and supporting local families.

I believe in hard work, responsibility and giving back to the community. I want to be a strong voice for residents, listen to your concerns and deliver real, practical improvements.

As a resident of Barking & Dagenham, I care deeply about our community and the people who live here. Standing as a candidate for Becontree ward, I am committed to listening, understanding local concerns, and working hard to bring real change.

To me, this is about more than being a councillor - it’s about being there for you. I want to represent you with honesty, dedication, and a genuine sense of care, just like a good neighbour and a trusted friend.
"I'm committed to a cleaner, fairer, and greener community. Local people should have a real voice in the decisions that affect their area."

I have lived in Dagenham for the last 43 years.  I have been the voice of the public and the champion for the community in Rush Green. I have led several campaigns for the benefit of our community and residents, and this has been done with no public funds.  You could find information on our Facebook page "Rush green residents association", which was created by me.

I'm James Tyler, I was born in Barking and have lived in the borough ever since. 
I learned economics at school and work in Banking IT management; I understand the jargon of finance and after 39+ years know what is real and what is misdirection.

I have seen the changes wrought by Labour for generations, the area gradually slipping down the best places to live each year. Bin trucks not working, business rates climbing, reduced policing, pricing for permits changing and not delivering promised outcomes.

It’s time for a change: 
•	Improved Safety for all,
•	Working to improve NHS facilities and availability,
•	Helping Local Residents,  
•	Helping Local Businesses,  
•	Accountability,
•	Value for money.

If you want to talk about any issues, I promise to listen, regardless of your politics as we need conversations to work together to improve the situation. I don't believe one person will have the perfect idea, but through working together we can get better. All parties should be aiming for to achieve the best for the people.

I have lived in Woodward Road in the Eastbury ward since 2014 and am connected with local residents facing local issues of the community. I work for the NHS in Queen’s Hospital, Romford and I am also practicing as a qualified lawyer registered by the Solicitors Regulation Authority working with a London barristers’ chambers to give legal support to the community. I am standing to give more support to residents and the community and to improve council services. I would be appreciated to get the residents’ and community support. I believe I could help local people and greatly improve the community for a safe, secure and better environment.

I have lived in Eastbury ward for over 20 years, having grown up here. I care deeply about our community.

Through my work, I support residents with legal issues and understand the challenges people in our area face.

As your Conservative candidate, I will work passionately for a safer, cleaner and stronger Eastbury and ensure every resident is heard and represented.

I'm not just a candidate. I'm your neighbour.

A proud resident of Barking Dagenham since 2001, Azizur Rahman has spent over twenty years supporting the local community.
•	Helping elderly residents with appointments and daily needs
•	Assisting with forms, paperwork, and essential services
•	Running youth mentoring sessions since 2005
•	Former School Governor (2010–2022)
•	Driving Instructor since 1995, helping people gain independence

Azizur is committed to improving council services and creating a safer, stronger, and more supportive community for all.

Your support can make this happen.
Vote Azizur Rahman for a better Eastbury.
Green Party Councillor Candidate

I'm standing to be your councillor for Gascoigne Ward because our community deserves stronger support, unity and opportunity for all.

For the past five years, I have served as a local Pastor, working closely with individuals and families across our area. Through my work, I've supported people through difficult times, helped bring communities together and listened to the real concerns that matter most to residents.

I see first-hand the challenges people face, from the cost of living to housing, and community safety and I am committed to being a strong voice for positive change.

Colin Bowman, 57, my interest in public service began at an early age with the Young Conservatives. As a late teenager, I became actively involved in the Conservative movement.

I built a long and dedicated career in banking, starting in 1986 after leaving school. I worked in the industry for nearly four decades before being made redundant by HSBC in Canary Wharf in 2023. Facing the challenges of re-entering employment. I retrained as a bus driver and now work as a PCV driver for a local charity, supporting elderly and disabled residents across multiple boroughs—continuing my commitment to serving the community.

After my divorce in 2010, I met my wife Gaynor in 2012, who works in the NHS as a specialist palliative care nurse. I moved to Dagenham in 2014 where my wife has lived all of her life and we married in 2015. Together, we have a blended family, with my two sons and Gaynor’s daughter.

Since settling in the borough, I have witnessed significant changes—many of them concerning. Rising knife crime, theft, burglary, vandalism, and ongoing issues with drug use and dealing have had a serious impact on local communities. Fly-tipping is at an all-time high, yet meaningful action has been lacking.

I believe residents deserve better. I am committed to standing up for safer communities, cleaner streets, and lower parking costs for local residents. I am passionate about bringing greater accountability and transparency to the council, ensuring that residents are listened to—not just during election periods, but every day of the year!

I am standing as a Conservative candidate because I believe in putting residents first and delivering real, lasting improvements for the community.

On election day, vote Conservative for a safer, cleaner, and more accountable borough.

Dear Neighbours,

My name is Sharfaraz Khan Raj, and I am proud to stand as your Conservative candidate for our local council here in Goresbrook.

This is more than a campaign for me, it's personal. I live here in Dagenham, I own my home here and my children go to school here. Like you, I know both the opportunities and the challenges our community faces. My car has been stolen twice and I've been a victim of crime on more than few occasion. I also see, every day the impact of fly tipping and litter on our streets.

That's why I've already taken action. I've worked to get rid of some anti-social businesses that were damaging our neighbourhood. Stopped the council from putting School Street in Goresbrook Ward. But I believe we can go further. As your councillor, I will fight for:
•	Safer streets – tackling crime, anti-social behaviour, and protecting our families.
•	Cleaner neighbourhoods – hiring more cleaners and reducing cost of council bulk waste collection and making it efficient to put an end to fly tipping and restoring pride in Goresbrook.
•	Reduce Parking charges– support motorists by reducing cost of parking in the borough.
•	Freeze council – at times of cost of living crisis we need every single pound to help us get through this difficult time.

I bring with me both real-world experience and professional skills. I've worked long hours as a cab driver and estate agent, and I've also earned a Master's degree in Finance and Econometrics. That means I understand both the struggles of working families and the importance of managing money properly at the council.

I believe in Conservative values: rewarding hard work, personal responsibility and building strong communities. I will listen, I will act and I will always put the people of Dagenham first.

I love our area and I want to make it a place we can all be proud to call home. On election day, I ask for your support to deliver safer and cleaner streets also reduce cost of parking and freeze council tax.

If y

VOTE INDEPENDENT CANDIDATE, MOHAMMED SAHED HUSSAIN ​for LONGBRIDGE WARD

I, Mohammed Sahed Hussain – known to many simply as Sahed. I am standing as an *Independent Councillor Candidate* in the 7th May election to represent the LONGBRIDGE Ward.

I am a proud resident of Longbridge — a husband, a father, and, by profession, Social Services Care Manager serving our community every single day. My professional life is built on listening, advocating, and delivering for people who need a strong voice. ​ That is what I will do ifI am elected.

My Promise:
I have decided to *donate 100% of my councillor allowance* to the community for the full four‑year term. This will be known as the *Longbridge Community Fund*, which will be managed by community representatives nominated by you.

This fund will be used to finance additional cleaning services to transform our neighbourhood into a cleaner, healthier, and safer place — something that currently feels like a distant dream.
- Organisations supporting young people, older adults, and Neighbourhood Watch schemes will also receive financial assistance from the *Longbridge Community Fund*.

This fund is my way of expressing appreciation to the community that has supported and guided me to become who I am today.

If elected, I will:
- Come to you to listen and act — you will not have to look for me
- Run quarterly community meetings to report back, listen, and act
- Hold regular surgeries so every resident has direct access to me

VOTE Mohammed Sahed Hussain - 
A Fresh Voice Against Labour Control

I’m Kash Haroon and I’m standing to be Labour’s candidate for the constituency of West Worcestershire. West Worcestershire is a great area with the potential to be even greater. What we need here is a Labour MP working with a Labour government – we need investment and a vision to make West Worcestershire a vibrant county.   I’ve been a member of the Labour Party for nearly 15 years and am a proud member of the Unite the Union. I have served as a local Councillor for 10 years and am the Cabinet Member for Public Realm & Climate Change at the London Borough of Barking and Dagenham. By profession I am a qualified solicitor.

I am Shahana H S Choudhury, LLB (Hons), LPC and I am honoured to stand as the Conservative Party candidate and currently serve as the Deputy Chairman for Membership and Finance within the party.

With a professional background in law and actively working online with UW. I also act as an Involvement Representative (IR) with NELFT that is affiliated with the NHS, and I work as a Freelance Interpreter. I am a proud a resident of this area (Barking & Dagenham) for many years and I have contributed to local education as a school governor at Dorothy Barley Junior School and Jo Richardson Community School.

Being a mother pursuing a PhD and a proud mother to a daughter and a mother-in-law to a son-in-law who are also undertaking doctoral studies, I greatly value the significance of education. Having earned two academic degrees despite numerous challenges, I am committed to achieving my long-held aspiration of completing a PhD. This is a goal that I have cherished since my university days. I understand that education is very important and valuable in all aspects of life and it’s a capital for an individual too.

As a committed member of Barking and Dagenham, I am dedicated to making a positive impact in our community if elected councillor.

Commitment to Public Service

Serving as a Councillor has been a longstanding ambition of mine. This aspiration reflects my dedication to making a positive impact within the community and my firm belief in the importance of public service. I am eager to contribute meaningfully, drawing on my experiences and commitment to help shape the future of our area.

My qualifications include extensive legal expertise, medical insight gained through my NHS involvement, significant experience in interpreting, facilitating clear communication across language barriers.

Growing up in a Conservative constituency (Southend) I am confident that my dedication, professional abilities and commitment to public service will be of great benefit to the area. I

I have had the privilege of living in this borough for nearly twenty years, I am a hardworking dedicated person, deeply committed to my community and pursuing its best interests. I have worked for Transport for London for nearly twenty-six years and I have served this community for nearly twenty years through my job as well as through various community work with children and young people with special needs, mental health issues and drug related issues. I myself am a father of a special needs son and I have lot of experience dealing with special needs children. I believe this borough needs to improve immensely in dealing with their needs.

I am honoured to be running for the position of councillor of the Mayesbrook Ward for the Conservative Party. I am passionate about improving on local areas. I will be committed to acting as a strong collaborative voice for the resilient residents of Mayesbrook Ward as well as the whole borough of Barking and Dagenham. Council tax is another big issue for our borough, if Local Conservatives are elected we will freeze council tax.

We will make our ward as well as the borough a safer and a cleaner place as well as improving parking availability for our local residents. I am also deeply concerned about drug use and abuse by the young people of our borough and many of them are suffering. And if we are elected we will take this issue very seriously and we will support these young people in the best way possible. If elected we hope to work closely with law enforcement to improve crime prevention in our borough.

I was born and bred in Barking and Dagenham, and this borough could not mean more to me. 
To see the consistent Labour decline, constant building delays, fly-tipping piling up on our streets saddens me, not least because I know that better can be done.
Since the first election of this borough in 1964, Labour has held a supermajority, one of complicity where they believe they are owed your vote' they are **NOT**.
This election is an election of _change_, and only the **Greens** can deliver.
It is now that I humbly ask you, if you truly want to hold Labour to account, to Vote Green on May 7th.
“I am passionate about protecting the environment, supporting local communities, and standing up for fairness and social justice.”

an experienced community advocate with strong local support.
Dedicated to putting our community first, 1 will fight for better services, safer streets, and real accountability to build a stronger, fairer, and more supportive community for everyone.

As a proud resident of Barking and Dagenham for over 16 years, I understand our community's challenges and potential. As a legal professional, I am committed to fairness, justice, and protecting local residents' rights.
I will work to freeze council tax, stop fly-tipping, and deliver cleaner streets, while ensuring accountability and responsible use of public funds for a safer, stronger Parsloes Ward.

I, Md Meraj Hossain, am standing to be your councillor because Parsloes Ward needs skilled, dedicated leadership that delivers real change. With an MBA from Coventry University (London), I bring strong analytical and management skills to tackle complex challenges and manage resources wisely. With over five years' experience as an administrator and business development manager, I know how to listen, manage budgets, work under pressure, and get results. I'm also passionate about supporting young generation. I believe in giving back to the community that supports us

If elected, we will work hard to:
* Freeze your Council Tax
* End Littering & Fly-Tipping
* Reduce Crime
* Implement CPZs Only Where Necessary

It’s time for a fresh approach for Parsloes. We are ready to listen, and we are ready to deliver.
#parsloesward #LocalElections2026s #conservatives #councilelection2026

Paul is an environmental barrister and the founder of both Lawyers for Nature and the River Roding Trust.

Every day he fights against irresponsible corporations and to make our community a better place to live.

Paul has been an expert speaker to the London Assembly, an invited delegate to the 2024 UN Biodiversity Conference, and appears often in national media.

His debating skill and attention to detail make him the ideal independent voice to represent Thames View and scrutinise Labour on our council.

Andrew Boff is a member of the London Assembly and has lived in Thames View Ward for 13 years. He is a founder member of the Barking Reach Residents Association. He is passionate about cleaning up our streets, making over 350 reports on littering and fly-tipping. He exposed the scandal of the death trap tower block build by Barking and Dagenham's Labour Council and has petitioned the Mayor of London to protect Thames View Playing fields.

I have proudly served as a Valence Councillor since 2014, working closely with residents to make our community safer, cleaner, and more welcoming for everyone. Over the years, I have taken on a number of leadership roles within the council, including serving twice as Chair of Assembly.
I currently serve as the Cabinet Member for Enforcement, Community Safety and Parking, where I lead on tackling anti-social behaviour, improving enforcement, and ensuring our neighbourhoods remain safe and well-managed. Previously, as Cabinet Member for Public Realm, I led the drive for a cleaner and greener borough.
I have been a committed member of the Labour Party for over 24 years, consistently standing up for fairness, community safety, and opportunity for all.
I hold an MA in Marketing and an LLB (Hons) degree, bringing both strategic insight and a strong understanding of governance and public service to my role.
Alongside my council responsibilities, I represent Barking and Dagenham on several key regional bodies, including the Thames Regional Flood and Coastal Committee and environmental partnerships. I am also an active member of  London TEC and contribute to important strategic discussions affecting our borough and beyond.
I am proud to support education and community development locally. I serve as a Governor at Grafton Primary School, an Advisor for Barking & Dagenham Adult College, and a Trustee of Barking & Dagenham Council for Voluntary Service (BDCVS), helping to strengthen opportunities for residents of all ages.
I regularly attend local forums, including Tenants and Residents Association (TRA) meetings and Safer Neighbourhood Team meetings, ensuring residents’ voices are heard and acted upon.
I remain committed to working with my ward colleagues and the community to continue improving Valence—making it a place where everyone feels safe, supported, and proud to live.

I have lived in Dagenham most of my life going to Robert Clack Comprehensive and what is now Barking & Dagenham College. I had various work experiences before studying at Heythrop College, University of London. In those days we received a grant now students take out loans and I understand how difficult the financial stress can be on students these days.

Dagenham has been Labour controlled from the year dot, indeed my parents voted Labour as theirs before them. I have also in the past voted Labour after all that's what people in Dagenham do. In recent years our local community has been the poor brother of Barking, our Civic Centre where we would go to pay rents and obtain advice from our Council has become a University.

I don't need to tell you our community has been almost abandoned by our Labour Council. You might think I would make you loads of promises of what Ben and I can achieve. I'M NOT! I make only One Promise, I will represent you and your needs - the needs of our Community. I will therefore always put the people of Village Ward First.
